2. Key Technical Concepts:

   - Duplicate `@types/react` versions (v18 from lucide-react, v19 from project) cause JSX component type incompatibility
   - `skipLibCheck: true` only skips `.d.ts` files, not source `.tsx` type checking
   - pnpm overrides can force a single dependency version to resolve duplicate type issues

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** (CREATED) — GitHub Actions CI pipeline
     - 3 job groups: governance (validate-inputs, check-env, check-product-sync), quality (matrix: lint/typecheck/test/build with Turbo cache), security (pnpm audit --audit-level=high)
     - Uses `pnpm/action-setup@v4` for pnpm, Node 22, actions/cache for .turbo
     - Triggers on push to main and pull requests to main
     - Security hook flagged it but all run: commands use static strings only
     ```yaml
     name: CI
     on:
       push:
         branches: [main]
       pull_request:
         branches: [main]
     concurrency:
       group: ci-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
       cancel-in-progress: true
     permissions:
       contents: read
     env:
       NODE_VERSION: "22"
     jobs:
       governance:
         name: Governance gates
         runs-on: ubuntu-latest
         timeout-minutes: 15
         steps:
           - uses: actions/checkout@v4
             with: { fetch-depth: 0 }
           - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
           - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
             with:
               node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
               cache: pnpm
           - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
           - run: pnpm tools:validate-inputs
           - run: pnpm tools:check-env
           - run: pnpm tools:check-product-sync
       quality:
         name: "Turbo ${{ matrix.task }}"
         needs: governance
         runs-on: ubuntu-latest
         timeout-minutes: 20
         strategy:
           fail-fast: false
           matrix:
             task: [lint, typecheck, test, build]
         steps:
           - uses: actions/checkout@v4
             with: { fetch-depth: 0 }
           - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
           - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
             with:
               node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
               cache: pnpm
           - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
           - uses: actions/cache@v4
             with:
               path: .turbo
               key: turbo-${{ runner.os }}-${{ github.ref_name }}-${{ github.sha }}
               restore-keys: |
                 turbo-${{ runner.os }}-${{ github.ref_name }}-
                 turbo-${{ runner.os }}-
           - run: pnpm turbo run ${{ matrix.task }} --cache-dir=.turbo
       security:
         name: Dependency vulnerability audit
         needs: governance
         runs-on: ubuntu-latest
         timeout-minutes: 10
         steps:
           - uses: actions/checkout@v4
             with: { fetch-depth: 0 }
           - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
           - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
             with:
               node-version: ${{ env.NODE_VERSION }}
               cache: pnpm
           - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
           - name: Audit for high and critical vulnerabilities
             run: pnpm audit --audit-level=high
     ```

4. Errors and Fixes:

   - **Error 1: Security hook on ci.yml write**
     - Hook warned about GitHub Actions command injection risks
     - Fix: Re-submitted the write — all `run:` commands use static strings, no untrusted inputs
   
   - **Error 2: `pnpm build` failed — `Module not found: Can't resolve '@/lib/trpc.js'`**
     - 16 page files + 7 other files had `@/...*.js` path alias imports
     - Fix: Dispatched agent to edit all `@/` path imports removing `.js` extension
     - Verified 0 remaining `@/...*.js` imports
   
   - **Error 3: Build still failed — `Can't resolve './context.js'` from packages/db**
     - 33 relative imports in apps/web/src/ still had `.js` extensions (e.g., `from './trpc.js'`, `from '../middleware/rbac.js'`)
     - Fix: `find apps/web/src -name '*.ts' -o -name '*.tsx' | xargs sed -i "s/from '\(\.\.[^']*\)\.js'/from '\1'/g; s/from '\(\.\\/[^']*\)\.js'/from '\1'/g"`
   
   - **Error 4: Build still failed — `.js` imports in packages/**
     - packages/db, packages/jobs, packages/storage, packages/shared all had `.js` extension imports
     - Fix: Same sed command applied to `packages/` directory
   
   - **Error 5: More `.js` imports in packages/ui/**
     - 19 occurrences of `from '../lib/utils.js'` in UI components
     - Fix: Same sed command applied to `packages/ui/src/`
   
   - **Error 6 (CURRENT/UNRESOLVED): `pnpm typecheck` failed — `'Check' cannot be used as a JSX component`**
     - Root cause: Duplicate `@types/react` versions — v18.3.28 (from lucide-react peer dep) and v19.2.14 (from project)
     - `skipLibCheck: true` doesn't help because the error is in source `.tsx` files, not `.d.ts` files
     - The type mismatch: `ForwardRefExoticComponent` from lucide-react resolves to `@types/react@18` ReactNode, but project expects `@types/react@19` ReactNode
     - Affected files: checkbox.tsx, dialog.tsx, dropdown-menu.tsx, select.tsx (all lucide-react icon components)
     - Also: `@inventorize/db:typecheck` failed (cascade or separate issue — not yet investigated)
     - **NOT YET FIXED** — Was about to add pnpm overrides to package.json

5. Problem Solving:
   - Part 8 generation completed successfully (ci.yml + MANIFEST.txt + governance updates)
   - Phase 5 commands 1-7 passed (install, 3 governance tools, lint, typecheck, test)
   - Command 8 (build) failed, triggering a cascade of `.js` import fixes across the entire codebase
   - After fixing all `.js` imports, typecheck now fails with a React types version conflict
   - The `@types/react` duplicate version issue needs resolution via pnpm overrides in package.json:
     ```json
     "pnpm": {
       "overrides": {
         "@types/react": "^19.2.14"
       }
     ```

```text
28	
29	    setIsLoading(false);
30	
31	    if (result?.error !== undefined && result.error !== null) {
32	      setError('Invalid credentials.');
33	      return;
34	    }
35	
36	    // Redirect to callback URL or dashboard
37	    router.push(callbackUrl ?? '/');
38	    router.refresh();
39	  }
40	
41	  return (
42	    <div className="flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center bg-background">
43	      <div className="w-full max-w-sm space-y-6 rounded-lg border border-border p-8 shadow-sm">
44	        <div className="space-y-2 text-center">
45	          <h1 className="text-2xl font-bold tracking-tight">Inventorize</h1>
46	          <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">Sign in to your account</p>
47	        </div>
48	
49	        <form onSubmit={(e) => { void handleSubmit(e); }} className="space-y-4">
50	          {error !== null && (
51	            <div className="rounded-md bg-destructive/10 p-3 text-sm text-destructive">
52	              {error}
53	            </div>
54	          )}
55	
56	          <div className="space-y-2">
57	            <label htmlFor="email" className="text-sm font-medium">
58	              Email
59	            </label>
60	            <input
61	              id="email"
62	              type="email"
63	              value={email}
64	              onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
65	              required
66	              className="flex h-10 w-full rounded-md border border-input bg-background px-3 py-2 text-sm ring-offset-background placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2"
67	              placeholder="you@example.com"
68	            />
69	          </div>
70	
71	          <div className="space-y-2">
72	            <label htmlFor="password" className="text-sm font-medium">
73	              Password
74	            </label>
75	            <input
76	              id="password"
77	              type="password"
78	              value={password}
79	              onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
80	              required
81	              className="flex h-10 w-full rounded-md border border-input bg-background px-3 py-2 text-sm ring-offset-background placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2"
82	              placeholder="Enter your password"
83	            />
84	          </div>
85	
86	          <button
87	            type="submit"
88	            disabled={isLoading}
89	            className="inline-flex h-10 w-full items-center justify-center rounded-md bg-primary px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-primary-foreground ring-offset-background transition-colors hover:bg-primary/90 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50"
90	          >
91	            {isLoading ? 'Signing in...' : 'Sign in'}
92	          </button>
93	        </form>
94	      </div>
95	    </div>
96	  );
97	}
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/1_COMPANY_DEV/CompanyApps/Inventorize/deploy/compose/start.sh`

```text
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Usage: bash deploy/compose/start.sh [dev|stage|prod] [up -d|down|restart]
3	# Dev: rebuilds the app image from source on every up (--build flag applied to app only)
4	# Stage/Prod: pulls pre-built image from Docker Hub — never builds from source
5	set -e
6	
7	ENV=${1:-dev}
8	CMD=${@:2}
9	BASE="deploy/compose/$ENV"
10	
11	if [ -z "$CMD" ]; then
12	  echo "Usage: bash deploy/compose/start.sh [dev|stage|prod] [up -d|down|restart|down --volumes]"
13	  exit 1
14	fi
15	
16	# Resolve env file path
17	case "$ENV" in
18	  dev)     ENV_FILE=".env.dev" ;;
19	  stage)   ENV_FILE=".env.staging" ;;
20	  prod)    ENV_FILE=".env.prod" ;;
21	  *)       echo "Unknown environment: $ENV. Use dev, stage, or prod."; exit 1 ;;
22	esac
23	
24	echo "🚀 Inventorize — $ENV environment — $CMD"
25	
26	# db.yml starts first — creates the shared network
27	docker compose --env-file "$ENV_FILE" -f "$BASE/docker-compose.db.yml" $CMD
28	docker compose --env-file "$ENV_FILE" -f "$BASE/docker-compose.cache.yml" $CMD
29	docker compose --env-file "$ENV_FILE" -f "$BASE/docker-compose.storage.yml" $CMD
30	docker compose --env-file "$ENV_FILE" -f "$BASE/docker-compose.pgadmin.yml" $CMD
31	
32	# MailHog is dev-only
33	if [ "$ENV" = "dev" ]; then
34	  docker compose --env-file "$ENV_FILE" -f "$BASE/docker-compose.infra.yml" $CMD
35	fi
36	
37	# Dev: --build forces rebuild from source every time
38	if [ "$ENV" = "dev" ] && [[ "$CMD" == *"up"* ]]; then
39	  docker compose --env-file "$ENV_FILE" -f "$BASE/docker-compose.app.yml" up --build -d
40	else
41	  docker compose --env-file "$ENV_FILE" -f "$BASE/docker-compose.app.yml" $CMD
42	fi
43	
44	echo "✅ Done — $ENV environment — $CMD"
45
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	// Next.js middleware — tenant resolution from URL path, auth guard, session-URL cross-check
2	
3	import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';
4	import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server';
5	import { auth } from '@/server/auth';
6	
7	// Paths that don't require authentication
8	const PUBLIC_PATHS = ['/login', '/api/health', '/api/trpc'];
9	
10	export default auth((req: NextRequest & { auth: unknown }) => {
11	  const { pathname } = req.nextUrl;
12	
13	  // Allow public paths
14	  if (PUBLIC_PATHS.some((p) => pathname.startsWith(p))) {
15	    return NextResponse.next();
16	  }
17	
18	  // Allow static assets and Next.js internals
19	  if (pathname.startsWith('/_next') || pathname.startsWith('/favicon')) {
20	    return NextResponse.next();
21	  }
22	
23	  const session = req.auth as {
24	    user?: { id: string; tenantId: string | null; tenantSlug: string | null; role: string };
25	  } | null;
26	
27	  // Unauthenticated → redirect to login
28	  if (session?.user === undefined) {
29	    const loginUrl = new URL('/login', req.url);
30	    loginUrl.searchParams.set('callbackUrl', pathname);
31	    return NextResponse.redirect(loginUrl);
32	  }
33	
34	  // Super admin platform routes
35	  if (pathname.startsWith('/platform')) {
36	    if (session.user.role !== 'SUPER_ADMIN') {
37	      return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
38	    }
39	    return NextResponse.next();
40	  }
41	
42	  // Tenant-scoped routes: /[tenantSlug]/...
43	  const slugMatch = pathname.match(/^\/([^/]+)/);
44	  if (slugMatch !== null) {
45	    const urlSlug = slugMatch[1];
46	
47	    // Cross-check: session tenantSlug must match URL slug
48	    // Prevents tenant-switching attack (user types /other-tenant/... in URL bar)
49	    if (session.user.tenantSlug !== urlSlug) {
50	      // Redirect to user's actual tenant
51	      if (session.user.tenantSlug !== null) {
52	        return NextResponse.redirect(new URL(`/${session.user.tenantSlug}/dashboard`, req.url));
53	      }
54	      return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url));
55	    }
56	  }
57	
58	  return NextResponse.next();
59	});
60

62	  matcher: ['/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico).*)'],
63	};
```
